content:
- Read the Ralph progress log, refreshed task summaries, and loaded choose-task rules from `.ralph/ralph-choose-task.md`.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks, found no active priority tags that outrank normal work, and resolved the only visible blocker (`story-22a/02` blocked by `story-22a/01`).
- Determined the repo is a Rust workspace (`runner`, `source-bootstrap`) plus a Go verify-service slice in `cockroachdb_molt`; the most logical next task is the next unblocked verify-image task: story 18 task 08 for `/metrics` progress/result exposure. If resuming later, continue from `.ralph/current_task.txt` and execute story 18 task 08 with the `tdd` skill.

- Read prior Ralph progress log and confirmed the current task pointer is story 18 task 08 about verify job progress/result metrics.
- Re-read the explicit `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions from disk as required for this turn.
- Next step is to inspect the task file and determine whether this turn must produce a plan or execute an existing approved plan.

- Opened story 18 task 08 and confirmed it is still at the pre-plan stage: no existing plan link and no `NOW EXECUTE` marker.
- Per task instructions, this turn must produce a plan file first, not implementation.
- Next I am reading the verify HTTP service code and surrounding tests so the new plan can specify public interfaces, TDD slices, and one concrete code-boundary cleanup.

- Read the story 18 task 08 file and determined this turn had to be planning-only because no existing plan or `NOW EXECUTE` marker was present.
- Inspected the current verify-service handler/runtime/tests plus verify reporter and legacy Prometheus code.
- Wrote the execution plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/08-task-expose-verify-job-progress-and-result-metrics_plans/2026-04-19-verify-job-progress-and-result-metrics-plan.md`.
- The plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE` because the interface decisions are concrete enough for the next turn.
- Key execution direction: keep `/metrics` on the existing verify-service mux, avoid exposing the default/global Prometheus registry, and split canonical per-job progress state away from the HTTP JSON DTO so both JSON and metrics derive from one internal snapshot.
- Next turn should execute that plan with strict red-green TDD slices, then run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` only.

- Read prior progress log and confirmed story 18 task 08 already has an approved plan ending in NOW EXECUTE.
- Re-read the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skills from disk for this turn.
- Next I am loading the task file plus the approved plan so execution follows the documented interface and stop conditions.

- Loaded the story 18 task 08 file and the approved execution plan.
- Confirmed this turn is execution, not planning: the plan already ends in NOW EXECUTE.
- Next I am reading the verify-service implementation and tests the plan references so I can start with the first red slice for GET /metrics.

- Read the current verifyservice service/http_test implementation and confirmed the core boundary smell: mutable internal progress state is stored directly in an HTTP DTO shape.
- Next I am reading the reporter, stats, and existing verify metrics packages to map the real report semantics before adding the service-local metrics collector.

- Confirmed the legacy verify metrics package uses the wrong prefix and default global Prometheus registration, so the new /metrics endpoint must be service-owned.
- A first package-test attempt from repo root failed because the verifyservice package lives under a nested Go module; next I am switching to the correct module root and starting the first failing /metrics test there.

- Added the first red test in verifyservice/http_test.go for GET /metrics exposing a running job-state sample with the required cockroach_migration_tool_verify_ prefix and job_id label.
- Next I am running only that test from the nested Go module with GOTOOLCHAIN=auto to capture the real red failure before implementing the route.

- Implemented the first green slice: GET /metrics is now registered on the verify-service mux via a service-local Prometheus registry and custom collector.
- The collector currently emits only cockroach_migration_tool_verify_job_state for known jobs; next I am rerunning the tracer-bullet test before broadening the contract.

- Added the next red integration test for a running job that emits a SummaryReport and must expose source_rows_total and checked_rows_total on /metrics, with the source database label derived from config.
- Next I am running only that test; it should fail because the service still has no canonical per-table progress snapshot and the collector currently emits only job_state.

- Refactored verifyservice to store canonical per-job progress snapshots separate from the HTTP DTO and render JSON from that snapshot.
- Added source_rows_total and checked_rows_total metrics from the snapshot and derived the source database label from the configured source URL.
- Next I am rerunning the targeted progress test plus a few existing job-JSON tests to confirm the refactor preserved existing behavior before adding more metric families.

- Added a red integration test proving repeated SummaryReport events for one table must collapse into one current source_rows_total sample and must also expose destination_rows_total plus mismatch-kind gauges.
- Next I am running only that slice; it should fail because the collector does not yet emit destination or mismatches metrics.

- Added the next red integration test for terminal metrics: a failed job must remain visible on /metrics with job_state{status="failed"} and errors_total.
- Next I am running only that slice; it should fail because the collector does not yet emit the errors_total family.

- Implemented destination_rows_total and mismatches_total from the canonical snapshot, plus errors_total for failed jobs.
- Next I am formatting the modified Go files and running the full verifyservice package tests to catch regressions before the repo-wide check/lint/test lanes.

- Removed the dead pre-refactor jobResult helper code so the canonical snapshot is the only mutable progress owner.
- Next I am running the required validation lanes starting with make check, alongside the verifyservice Go package tests because the root Makefile only covers the Rust workspace.

- Validation so far is green: make check passed and the full verifyservice Go package tests passed from the nested Go module.
- Next I am running make lint and make test, still not touching make test-long because this task is not a story-end lane.

- make check and make lint passed, but make test found a real failure in the default bootstrap verify-image contract lane.
- The failure indicates my snapshot refactor changed the JSON verify audit surface too much: the e2e test now sees only a collapsed summary stream and no mismatch rows where the contract still expects the richer result payload.
- Next I am reading the failing Rust-side audit assertions so I can preserve the existing JSON boundary while keeping the new metrics backed by the canonical snapshot.

- Fixed the failing real verify-image contract by restoring the JSON summary event stream inside the canonical snapshot while keeping /metrics derived from the latest per-table snapshot.
- Next I am rerunning the exact failing Rust e2e contract test plus the full verifyservice Go package tests to verify both boundaries together.

- Fixed the real verify-image contract regression by preserving the JSON summary event stream while metrics stay derived from the latest per-table snapshot.
- The exact failing Rust contract test now passes, and I am rerunning the final required validation lanes on the final tree: make check, make lint, make test, plus nested Go verifyservice tests.

- Final validation rerun is mostly green on the final tree: make check passed, make lint passed, and nested Go verifyservice tests passed.
- Next I am rerunning make test after the contract regression fix so the task gate is green end to end.

- Added the explicit label-discipline / no-free-text metrics test that parses the Prometheus exposition and locks the family label sets.
- Next I am formatting the updated Go test file and rerunning the full verifyservice package tests before the final task-switch and git steps.

- Added the explicit metrics label-discipline test and reran the full nested Go verifyservice package successfully.
- Final task state is ready: next I am checking git status, running Ralph task_switch.sh as required, then committing and pushing all task files plus code changes.
